title: potentilla parryi var. wilderae
intro: Potentilla parryi var. wilderae, also known as Wild Buckthorn or Wild Potentilla, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Potentilla parryi var. wilderae include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be taken orally or applied topically to the skin.
